what: Validation of the v1 home-grown all-chain CA-lDDT (complex truth) against gold-standard DockQ, on the 40 v1 complex targets. Boltz-2 predictions vs RCSB references, DockQ v2 (bioconda) automatic chain mapping.
interpretation: The report's complex 'calibration collapse' (pLDDT vs CA-lDDT = 0.16) is largely a CA-lDDT metric artifact: pLDDT vs gold-standard DockQ = 0.44, and the home-grown CA-lDDT agrees with DockQ at only 0.30 (it is intra-chain-dominated, as flagged in adversarial review).
The specialist DOES emit a well-calibrated interface confidence: ipTM vs DockQ = 0.77. The v1 report under-sold complex calibration by using pLDDT as the cue and CA-lDDT as truth.
Implication for the redo: score complexes with DockQ (done here) and use ipTM (not pLDDT) as the complex reliability signal.
